title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: vignette walks worked Rasch analysis nine-item Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9; Kroenke et al. (2001)) using easyRasch2 package. example follows four psychometric criteria proposed Christensen et al. (2021) validation patient-reported outcome measures (PROMs): Unidimensionality — items measure single latent construct. Local independence — conditioning latent trait, item responses independent . Ordered response category thresholds (monotonicity) — moving latent trait increases probability higher categories. Invariance / DIF — item parameters across relevant external groups (e.g. gender). move two complementary descriptors commonly reported alongside four criteria : Targeting — well person item locations overlap latent continuum. Reliability — precisely scale separates respondents. extensive treatment Rasch analysis R, see https://pgmj.github.io/raschrvignette/RaschRvign.html. Bayesian sibling package, see https://pgmj.github.io/easyRaschBayes/ NOTE: simulation-based functions use low number iterations make vignette render faster. use iterations actual analysis work. methods, 500-1000 useful, except conditional infit, 100-400 optimal, depending sample size (Johansson 2025).

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: bundled phq9 dataset 600-respondent random subsample PHQ-9 module U.S. National Health Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES, September 2024 release) complete responses nine items. NHANES microdata released public domain U.S. federal government. plot chunk data-overview
code: library(easyRasch2) data(phq9) items <- phq9[, paste0("q", 1:9)]   # 9 item columns, scored 0..3 gender <- phq9$gender               # external grouping variables age <- phq9$age                     #  # add item information item_desc <- c(   "Little interest or pleasure in doing things",   "Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless",   "Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much",   "Feeling tired or having little energy", "Poor appetite or overeating",   "Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down",   "Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television",   "Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed?",   "Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way" )  item_resp <- c("Not at all","Several days","More than \nhalf the days","Nearly every day") str(items) #> 'data.frame':    600 obs. of  9 variables: #>  $ q1: int  3 0 1 2 3 3 1 3 2 1 ... #>  $ q2: int  3 0 2 3 3 3 1 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q3: int  3 1 3 0 3 1 0 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q4: int  3 1 3 2 3 3 1 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q5: int  3 0 3 2 3 2 0 1 2 0 ... #>  $ q6: int  3 2 3 2 3 2 2 3 3 0 ... #>  $ q7: int  3 3 3 2 3 2 2 3 3 0 ... #>  $ q8: int  1 0 2 0 3 3 0 0 1 0 ... #>  $ q9: int  3 0 0 2 3 1 2 0 0 0 ... summary(rowSums(items)) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>    0.00   10.00   16.00   15.41   21.00   27.00 hist(rowSums(items), col = "lightblue", main = "Histogram of ordinal sum scores") table(gender, useNA = "ifany") #> gender #> Female   Male   <NA>  #>    426    143     31 summary(age) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   15.00   26.00   33.00   36.06   44.00   85.00

text: fitting model worth eyeballing response distributions: Figure 1. Faceted bar chart response distributions Figure 2. Response distribution tile plot Figure 3. Stacked-bar response distribution
code: RMplotBar(items, ncol = 2) RMplotTile(items, category_labels = item_resp) RMplotStackedbar(items, show_percent = TRUE)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: easyRasch2 provides several complementary unidimensionality diagnostics can combined robust conclusion: item-level conditional infit MSQ statistics (Müller 2020) item-level item-restscore associations Goodman-Kruskal’s gamma (Kreiner 2011) confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) WLSMV estimator ordinal data principal components analysis (PCA) standardised residuals (Chou Wang 2010) Martin-Löf test Monte-Carlo p-values (Christensen Kreiner 2007)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Conditional item infit mean-square statistics flag items whose response patterns deviate Rasch expectation. RMitemInfitCutoff(), per-item highest-density intervals serve reference instead rule--thumb cutoffs (Johansson 2025). MSQ values based conditional estimation (n = 600 complete cases). Cutoff values based 100 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI). can also get plot summarizing simulated observed item infit, using RMitemInfitCutoffPlot(). Since conditional infit needs complete data, sibling function uses multiple imputation infit useful partial missingness data - RMitemInfitMI() RMitemInfitCutoffMI(). important note RIitemfit() function uses conditional infit, robust different sample sizes makes ZSTD unnecessary (Müller 2020). Müller also questions usefulness outfit, simulation study (Johansson 2025) reached conclusion. Thus, outfit reported unless requested. low item fit value, often referred item “overfit” Rasch model, indicates responses may predictable. often case items general/broad scope relation latent variable, instance asking feeling depressed depression questionnaire. often find overfitting items also residual correlations (local dependencies) items. Overfit may likened much stronger factor loading items confirmatory factor analysis higher level discrimination Item Response Theory model two parameters. high item fit value, often referred “underfit” Rasch model, can indicate several things. Often underfit due multidimensionality question difficult interpret thus noisy response data. latter instance caused question asks two things time, ambiguous reasons. Next visual presentation conditional item fit across latent continuum, respondents split groups based total score. Figure 4. Conditional ICCs five class intervals
code: infit_cut <- RMitemInfitCutoff(items, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                            seed = 3) RMitemInfit(items, cutoff = infit_cut) RMitemICCPlot(items, class_intervals = 5)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Item-restscore uses Goodman-Kruskal’s gamma shows expected observed correlation item score based rest items (Kreiner 2011). Similarly, inverted, item infit, lower observed correlation value expected indicates underfit, item may belong dimension. higher expected observed value indicates overfitting possibly redundant item. Overfitting items often also show issues local dependency. Compared infit, item-restscore often flags overfit items (based experience), less often flags underfit items (based simulation study (Johansson 2025)).
code: RMitemRestscore(items)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: RMdimCFACutoff() fits unidimensional ordinal CFA observed data data simulated unidimensional PCM, returns parametric-bootstrap cut-offs model fit indices SRMR, CFI RMSEA. Model fit values exceed simulated cut-values extreme plausible unidimensional data generating process. Partial Credit Model posterior-predictive CFA fit-index check. Observed CFA fit (one-factor, lavaan WLSMV, ordered = TRUE) vs simulated null distribution PCM unidimensionality. n = 600 complete cases, 9 items, 100 parametric-bootstrap iterations. Cutoffs one-sided 99th percentile simulated distribution; item flagged observed value lies worst 1% null distribution unfavourable direction.
code: cfa_cut <- RMdimCFACutoff(items, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                        seed = 2) cfa_cut

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: fitting Rasch model, residuals contain systematic structure. largest eigenvalue residual correlation matrix can considered headline diagnostic; value clearly simulation-based cut-suggest secondary dimension. However, eigenvalue largest value support unidimensionality. Partial Credit Model (600 complete cases, 9 items). Total observed variance: 54.6% explained measures, 45.4% unexplained (basis PCA; n = 600 non-extreme cases). First-contrast cutoff = 1.303 based 100 simulation iterations (99th percentile). Also interest plot item standardised loadings first residual contrast item locations. figure can helpful identify clusters data, perhaps related local dependency /multidimensionality. Figure 5. Standardised loadings first residual contrast
code: pca_cut <- RMdimResidualPCACutoff(items, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                        seed = 1) RMdimResidualPCA(items, cutoff = pca_cut) RMdimResidualPCA(items, output = "loadings")

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Local independence (LD) can assessed multiple methods. Yen’s Q_3 statistic (Yen 1984) correlation person-item standardised residuals every item pair. Pair-wise Q_3 values simulation-based cut-flag LD (Christensen et al. 2017). Dynamic cut-: 0.037 (mean Q3 = -0.114 + 0.152). Correlations exceeding cut-may indicate local dependence. powerful Q_3 test, one can use simulated cutoffs object plot expected range residual correlations item-pair compare observed value. ’ll limit output 6 item-pairs deviate . plot chunk plot_q3 second perspective LD partial gamma coefficient (Kreiner Christensen 2004; Kreiner 2007) observed item pairs, conditional rest-score. Note function evaluates directions LD, thus output two tables. ’ll restrict output 6 item-pairs largest LD deviations. Partial gamma LD analysis (n = 600 complete cases). Positive gamma indicates positive local dependence items. Showing top 6 36 pairs |gamma|. Direction 1: rest score = total - Item2. Partial gamma LD analysis (n = 600 complete cases). Positive gamma indicates positive local dependence items. Showing top 6 36 pairs |gamma|. Direction 2: rest score = total - Item1. can also get simulation-based thresholds partial gamma LD, using RMlocdepGammaCutoff(), can used RMlocdepGamma() also plot results RMlocdepGammaPlot() Item pairs flagged Q_3 partial gamma strongest candidates inspection possible item revision.
code: q3_cut <- RMlocdepQ3Cutoff(items, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                            seed = 4) RMlocdepQ3(items, cutoff = q3_cut) RMlocdepQ3Plot(simfit = q3_cut, data = items, n_pairs = 6) RMlocdepGamma(items, n_pairs = 6)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: polytomous item measuring intended, thresholds separating adjacent response categories ordered: threshold “” “Several days” sit one “Several days” “half days”, . classical method assess item response functions plot probability response curves item response category. plot chunk ipf_plot RMitemHierarchy() plots item’s threshold locations latent scale, ordered overall item difficulty. Disordered thresholds appear overlapping reversed segments clear signal response categories used intended order. Figure 6. Item-hierarchy
code: RMitemCatProb(items, category_labels = item_resp) RMitemHierarchy(items, item_labels = item_desc)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: use two complementary DIF assessments. Andersen likelihood-ratio test (LRT, Andersen 1973) partitions sample external variable, refits model subgroup, compares item locations. partial gamma approach (Kreiner 2007; Christensen et al. 2021) looks association item responses external variable conditional rest-score. run gender variable (dropping respondents missing gender):
code: keep    <- !is.na(gender) items_g <- items[keep, ] gender_g <- droplevels(gender[keep])

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Figure 7. Andersen LR-test DIF locations gender plot shows item threshold locations estimated gender group corresponding confidence band.
code: RMdifLR(items_g, dif_var = gender_g, level = "threshold")

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Partial gamma DIF analysis (n = 569 complete cases). Positive gamma indicates higher scores higher DIF group levels. model-based DIF analysis can handle continuous covariates interactions (e.g. age × gender), see ?RMdifTree (Strobl et al. 2015; Henninger et al. 2025).
code: RMdifGamma(items_g, dif_var = gender_g)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: targeting plot summarises well item-threshold distribution matches distribution person locations latent scale — Wright-map style display. Figure 8. Person-item targeting
code: RMtargeting(items)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: RMreliability() reports four reliability metrics: person separation reliability (PSI); Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) estimate derived posterior person-location uncertainty using plausible values; Cronbach’s alpha; Empirical reliability (using mirt::empirical_rxx(). PSI, alpha empirical can use bootstrap confidence intervals. reliability metrics range 0 1, higher values indicating better separation/precision. Reliability 9 items, n = 600. PSI excludes min/max scoring respondents. converting ordinal sum-scores interval-scaled person-location estimates associated standard errors, use RMscoreSE(). Person locations via Warm’s WLE (CML item parameters eRm). RMscoreSE() also option EAP scores (expected á posteriori).
code: RMreliability(items, draws = 200, rmu_iter = 20, parallel = FALSE,               seed = 5) RMscoreSE(items, output = "figure") #> Error in `match.arg()`: #> ! 'arg' should be one of "kable", "dataframe", "ggplot" RMscoreSE(items)

text: easyRasch2 CRAN-targeted R package Rasch measurement theory analysis workflows. successor easyRasch, offering lightweight, CRAN-ready structure proper namespacing minimal dependencies. central design choice simulation-based critical values various fit statistics. Rather relying rule--thumb cutoffs, diagnostics paired parametric-bootstrap function generates empirical null distribution fitted Rasch / PCM model observed sample. broader Rasch-analysis tutorials, see vignette sibling package easyRasch.

title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
text: Estimation: Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) via eRm, psychotools, iarm item parameters; Weighted Likelihood Estimation (WLE) person parameters; mirt (MML) CML alternatives exist (e.g., Q3 residual correlations). Output: knitr::kable() tables (Quarto-friendly), ggplot2 figures, "dataframe" output options downstream use. Naming: Functions use RM prefix (e.g., RMlocdepQ3()).

title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
code: library(easyRasch2) data("pcmdat2", package = "eRm") options(mc.cores = 4) set.seed(42)  # Conditional item infit with simulation-based cutoffs simfit <- RMitemInfitCutoff(pcmdat2, iterations = 250) RMitemInfit(pcmdat2, cutoff = simfit)  # Test of unidimensionality via posterior-predictive ordinal CFA cfa_res <- RMdimCFACutoff(pcmdat2, iterations = 250) cfa_res                # kable: observed vs simulated cutoffs RMdimCFAPlot(cfa_res)     # histogram + observed diamond  # DIF analysis via Andersen's LR test grp <- factor(sample(c("A", "B"), nrow(pcmdat2), replace = TRUE)) RMdifLR(pcmdat2, dif_var = grp)  # Rasch-tree DIF with effect-size classification on continuous + # categorical covariates simultaneously covs <- data.frame(   group = grp,   band  = sample(c("low", "high"), nrow(pcmdat2), replace = TRUE) ) RMdifTree(pcmdat2, covariates = covs)

title: Partial Gamma DIF Analysis — RMdifGamma
text: Partial gamma (Bjorner et al., 1998) measures association item response exogenous grouping variable, controlling total score. Values near 0 indicate DIF. Recommended interpretive thresholds (Bjorner et al., 1998): negligible DIF: gamma within \([-0.21, 0.21]\), gamma significantly different 0. Slight moderate DIF: gamma within \([-0.31, 0.31]\) (outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\)), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). Moderate large DIF: gamma outside \([-0.31, 0.31]\), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Simulation-Based Partial Gamma DIF Cutoff Determination — RMdifGammaCutoff
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). complete cases (rows without NA) used. dif_var vector (factor, character, integer) defining group membership DIF analysis. Must length nrow(data). actual group labels used determine number groups relative sizes; simulation, respondents randomly assigned groups proportions, true DIF construction. iterations Integer. Number simulation iterations (default 250). parallel Logical. Use parallel processing via mirai available (default TRUE). n_cores Integer NULL. Number parallel workers. title: Simulation-Based Partial Gamma DIF Cutoff Determination — RMdifGammaCutoff

Bounds computed using method specified cutoff_method. actual_iterations Number successful iterations. sample_n Number complete cases used. sample_summary Summary statistics estimated person parameters. item_names Character vector item names data. dif_group_sizes Named integer vector group sizes used simulation (matches proportions observed dif_var). cutoff_method method used compute cutoffs ("hdci" "quantile"). hdci_width HDCI width used (meaningful cutoff_method = "hdci").

title: Simulation-Based Partial Gamma DIF Cutoff Determination — RMdifGammaCutoff
text: simulation iteration function: Resamples person parameters (thetas) replacement ML estimates. Simulates item response data Rasch model (dichotomous via psychotools::rrm() polytomous via internal partial credit simulator). Creates random DIF variable sampling group labels proportions observed dif_var, true DIF construction. Computes partial gamma DIF statistics via iarm::partgam_DIF(). distribution partial gamma values across iterations provides empirical critical values per item. Values real data fall outside bounds suggest DIF exceeds expected chance correctly fitting Rasch model. Failed iterations (e.g., due convergence issues degenerate data) silently discarded. Supports dichotomous data (via eRm::RM() psychotools::rrm()) polytomous data (via eRm::PCM() internal partial credit score simulator). Parallel processing provided mirai package (optional). Install install.packages("mirai") enable parallelisation. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

what: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: Splits Rasch model external grouping variable using eRm::LRtest() reports per-group item locations (per-group threshold locations) together standard errors. single function replaces four legacy helpers (RIdifTableLR, RIdifThreshTblLR, RIdifFigureLR, RIdifThreshFigLR) exposing two underlying axes – level (item threshold) output (data.frame, kable, ggplot) – arguments. data preparation pipeline feeds six combinations.

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
code: RMdifLR(   data,   dif_var,   model = c("auto", "PCM", "RM"),   level = c("item", "threshold"),   output = c("ggplot", "kable", "dataframe"),   cutoff = 0.5,   conf = 0.95,   sort = FALSE )

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: data data.frame matrix item responses (non-negative integers, 0-based). One column per item, one row per person. Person IDs grouping variables must included – pass grouping variable separately via dif_var. dif_var Vector length nrow(data) (factor, character, numeric) defining DIF grouping variable. Coerced factor; unused levels dropped. Rows dif_var NA dropped message. Must result least 2 groups cleaning. model One "auto" (default), "PCM", "RM". "auto" fits eRm::RM() data dichotomous (max response = 1) eRm::PCM() otherwise. "RM" errors polytomous data. level One "item" (default) "threshold". "item" reports item's mean threshold location per group; "threshold" reports individual threshold per group. dichotomous (RM) data two views equivalent (one threshold per item). output One "ggplot" (default), "kable", "dataframe". data.frame view always carries Flagged logical column; kable view bolds flagged rows; ggplot view shows confidence intervals. cutoff Numeric NULL. Threshold (logits) Flagged column: row flagged MaxDiff > cutoff, MaxDiff difference largest smallest per-group location item (threshold). Set NULL suppress flagging. Default 0.5. conf Numeric (0, 1). Confidence level used ggplot error bars. Default 0.95. sort Logical. kable output : sort rows MaxDiff (descending). Default FALSE.

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: data.frame, knitr_kable object, ggplot object, depending output. data.frame one row per item (level = "item") per item x threshold (level = "threshold"), columns Item (Threshold threshold level), one numeric column per group level, column unsplit fit, MaxDiff, Flagged (cutoff non-NULL), matching SE_* columns. Andersen LR test result attached attr(result, "lr_test") data.frame, kable footnote, ggplot caption (LR \(\chi^2\), df, p-value).

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: Partial Credit Model (PCM) fitted default polytomous data dichotomous Rasch Model (RM) fitted responses 0/1; can overridden via model. data.frame kable outputs, locations reported centred eRm parameterisation returned eRm::thresholds(). Per-group fits come eRm::LRtest(..., splitcr = dif_var); unsplit fit (column) model fitted full dataset. Andersen LR statistic, df, p-value reported lr_test attribute / caption come directly LRtest()'s return value. cell_spec()-style HTML cell colouring used legacy easyRasch package dropped favour logical Flagged column (bold rendering kable output), kable renders correctly HTML, LaTeX, pipe/markdown.

what: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
text: Detects Differential Item Functioning (DIF) recursively splitting sample one covariates using psychotree::raschtree() (dichotomous data) psychotree::pctree() (polytomous data), computes per-split effect-size measures every item – Mantel-Haenszel odds-ratio (ETS Delta scale) dichotomous data, partial gamma coefficient polytomous data – classifies ETS /B/C categories. Optionally, iterative purification step (Holland & Thayer, 1988; Bjorner et al., 1998) applied, tree stability across resamples can assessed via stablelearner::stabletree().

title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
code: RMdifTree(   data,   covariates,   model = c("auto", "PCM", "RM"),   effect_size = c("auto", "MH", "pgamma"),   purification = c("none", "iterative"),   p_adj = c("none", "fdr", "bonferroni"),   thresholds = c(0.21, 0.31),   alpha = 0.05,   prune_negligible = FALSE,   stability = FALSE,   stability_B = 100L,   stability_sampler = c("subsampling", "bootstrap"),   min_n_per_level = 20L,   on_rescale = c("message", "warning", "stop"),   output = c("kable", "dataframe", "tree", "plot"),   ... )

title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
text: data data.frame matrix item responses (non-negative integers, 0-based). One column per item. Items – pass covariates separately via covariates. covariates data.frame nrow(data) rows giving DIF covariates. Columns may numeric (continuous), factor, ordered factor, logical. least one column required. model One "auto" (default), "PCM", "RM". "auto" picks RM data dichotomous (max response = 1) PCM otherwise. effect_size One "auto" (default), "MH" (Mantel-Haenszel), "pgamma" (partial gamma). "auto" uses MH RM partial gamma PCM. purification "none" (default) "iterative". Iterative purification recomputes effect size excluding items already classified DIF matching score (Bjorner et al., 1998). p_adj Multiple-testing adjustment partial-gamma classification: "none" (default), "fdr" (Benjamini & Hochberg), "bonferroni". Ignored MH (uses ETS test directly). thresholds Numeric length-2 vector partial-gamma B/C boundaries (default c(0.21, 0.31)). Ignored MH (ETS Delta-scale boundaries 1.0 / 1.5 used). alpha Significance level /C tests. Default 0.05. prune_negligible Logical. TRUE, splits whose every item classified pruned tree. Default FALSE. stability Logical. TRUE, runs stablelearner::stabletree() fitted tree assess variable-selection cutpoint stability across resamples. Default FALSE. stability_B Integer. Number resamples stability assessment. Default 100. stability_sampler One "subsampling" (default) "bootstrap". Subsampling recommended tree stability bootstrap resamples can drop levels categorical covariates, breaking model fit small subsamples. min_n_per_level Integer. Minimum count required factor level covariates. Default 20. Set 0 disable. on_rescale One "message" (default), "warning", "stop". Controls function reports situation one items responses lowest category within terminal node tree (psychotree silently rescales items per node, leaves per-node item parameters different metric). MH partial-gamma effect sizes reported computed raw responses affected. diagnostic concerns per-node item parameters visible via plot(tree). output One "kable" (default), "dataframe", "tree", "plot". "kable" renders per-split per-item effect-size table knitr::kable(); "dataframe" returns underlying tidy data.frame; "tree" returns augmented partykit tree object; "plot" returns partykit tree plot item names terminal-node x-axis (tp_args = list(names = TRUE)). full control plot, use output = "tree" call plot() result tp_args. stability = TRUE, stability summary attached attr(result, "stability") (small data.frame) attr(result, "stability_kable") (pre-rendered kable). ... Additional arguments forwarded psychotree::raschtree() psychotree::pctree(), e.g. minsize, alpha parameter-instability test. Note: alpha (tree-fitting argument) independent alpha ETS classification ; pass via ....

title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
text: Depending output: "kable" knitr_kable object one row per (split node x item), grouped node via pack_rows-style section headers. caption summarises model, effect-size measure, purification, (requested) stability. "dataframe" data.frame one row per (split node x item): columns NodeID, Split (human-readable description split), Variable, Direction, Item, EffectSize, SE, Class (/B/C), Flagged (TRUE B C), n_left, n_right. "tree" fitted partykit tree object class c("RMdifTree", ...), effect-size results stored tree$info$effectsize. "plot" plotted partykit tree. Stability results (stability = TRUE) attached return value attr(result, "stability") (data.frame) attr(result, "stability_kable") (pre-rendered kable).

title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
text: Continuous covariates (e.g., age years) interactions among multiple covariates handled natively model-based recursive-partitioning machinery partykit / psychotree. ETS Delta-scale Mantel-Haenszel. title: Martin-Lof Test of Unidimensionality — RMdimMartinLof
text: routine screening tool. test requires priori partition items subscales; using post-hoc , e.g., partition suggested RMdimResidualPCA()'s PC1 sign inflate Type-error rate. source papers state explicitly. Test statistic. items partitioned D subscales, total score \(t\) subscores \((t_1, \ldots, t_D)\) (Christensen et al. 2002, eq. 22): $$T = 2\Bigl[\sum_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}   n_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}\log(n_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}/N)   - \sum_t n_t\log(n_t/N)   - \ell_C(\hat{\epsilon}) + \sum_d \ell_C(\hat{\epsilon}^{(d)})\Bigr]$$ \(\ell_C\) conditional log-likelihood \(\hat{\epsilon}^{(d)}\) CML estimates d-th subscale alone. CML fits use psychotools::raschmodel() (RM) psychotools::pcmodel() (PCM) speed. Monte Carlo sampling H0. Following Christensen & Kreiner (2007): () sample N total scores empirical score distribution \(n_t/N\); (b) sampled score, sample item-response vector conditional distribution \(p(x \mid t, \hat{\epsilon})\) given eq. 4 paper. dichotomous data fast algorithm Christensen & Kreiner (2007, p. 23) used (sample without replacement weighted item easinesses). polytomous data recursive \(\gamma\)-function approach used, item's response sampled conditional remaining items' joint score distribution (computed via psychotools::elementary_symmetric_functions()). Iterations fail (e.g., simulated dataset empty category item) silently dropped. Item parameters estimated observed data held fixed across MC iterations. Christensen & Kreiner (2007) use extended likelihood function (Tjur, 1982) empirical score distribution non-parametric estimate latent distribution, distributional assumption \(\theta\) needed.

title: Standardised Residuals from the Joint Subscore Distribution — RMdimMartinLofResiduals
text: cell joint subscore table (indexed \((t_1, \ldots, t_D)\)), conditional probability H0 given total score \(t = \sum_d t_d\) $$p(t_1, \ldots, t_D \mid t) = \prod_d \gamma^{(d)}_{t_d} / \gamma_t,$$ expected count \(e = n_t \cdot p\), residual \((o - e) / \sqrt{n_t \cdot p \cdot (1 - p)}\). CML estimates unidimensional model used \(\gamma\)-functions. Reading table (D = 2): unidimensional null, residuals patternless roughly N(0, 1). Multidimensionality positively correlated dimensions typically shows positive residuals corners antidiagonal (high t1 + low t2, low t1 + high t2) negative residuals near antidiagonal centre (matched subscores). Negatively correlated dimensions show positive residuals table corners (high/low low/high) negative residuals high/high low/low. See Christensen et al. (2002, section7) worked example. Cells total score observed cases (n_t = 0) uninformative dropped output.

what: Simulation-based Cutoff for First-Contrast Eigenvalue — RMdimResidualPCACutoff
title: Simulation-based Cutoff for First-Contrast Eigenvalue — RMdimResidualPCACutoff
text: Parametric bootstrap producing empirical upper percentile largest eigenvalue PCA standardized residuals correctly fitting Rasch model. iteration function resamples theta values data-based estimates, simulates response data, refits appropriate Rasch model, extracts first-contrast eigenvalue. Several upper-tail percentiles resulting distribution returned; 99th percentile reported suggested cutoff (matching convention used RMlocdepQ3Cutoff).

title: Simulation-based Cutoff for First-Contrast Eigenvalue — RMdimResidualPCACutoff
text: Rule--thumb cutoffs first-contrast eigenvalue depend strongly sample size, test length, item-parameter spread; simulation-based cutoffs tailored data defensible (Chou & Wang, 2010). Per iteration: theta values sampled replacement WLE/MLE estimates derived fitted full-sample model; response data simulated model (psychotools::rrm dichotomous, partial-credit simulator polytomous); model refitted eRm::RM() / eRm::PCM(); standardized residuals extracted via eRm::itemfit()$st.res; prcomp() run; first-contrast eigenvalue recorded. Iterations fail (e.g., due degenerate simulated dataset category represented) silently dropped. iarm package required.

title: Simulation-based Cutoff for First-Contrast Eigenvalue — RMdimResidualPCACutoff
text: Chou, Y.-T., & Wang, W.-C. (2010). Checking dimensionality item response models principal component analysis standardized residuals. Educational Psychological Measurement, 70(5), 717-731. doi:10.1177/0013164410379322

title: Simulation-based Cutoff for First-Contrast Eigenvalue — RMdimResidualPCACutoff

title: Item Category Probability Curves — RMitemCatProb

Missing values (NA) allowed — model fit handles . item_labels Optional character vector descriptive item labels (facet strip titles). Must length ncol(data). NULL (default), column names used. category_labels Optional character vector labels response categories (legend). Must length number categories spanning 0 maximum observed value. NULL (default), numeric category values used. theta_range Numeric length 2. Range latent trait \(\theta\) (logits) plotted along x-axis. NULL (default) range c(-4, 4) label_curves = "legend" wider c(-5, 5) label_curves = "path" — wider range gives geomtextpath horizontal room place per-curve labels peaks. Pass explicit c(lo, hi) override. n_points Integer. Number evenly-spaced \(\theta\) values curves evaluated. Default 200L. viridis_option Character. Viridis palette identifier. One "" "H". Default "D" (viridis green). viridis_end Numeric (0, 1]. Upper end viridis palette range; lower values keep palette inside mid-tones (avoids bright yellow 1.0). Either "ggplot" (default) ggplot object, "dataframe" underlying long-format probability table. label_curves Character. response categories identified. "legend" (default) draws lines separate colour legend one swatch per category — standard multi-item presentation. Requires optional geomtextpath package. item Character integer. Used label_curves = "path". Either item name (matching column data) column index, identifying item's category curves plot. title: Item Category Probability Curves — RMitemCatProb
text: polytomous item response categories \(0, 1, \ldots, K_i\) threshold parameters \(\delta_{,1}, \ldots, \delta_{,K_i}\) eRm::thresholds(), PCM category probability $$P(X_i = k \mid \theta) = \frac{\exp(\sum_{j=1}^{k}   (\theta - \delta_{,j}))}{\sum_{k'=0}^{K_i} \exp(\sum_{j=1}^{k'}   (\theta - \delta_{,j}))},$$ empty sum (\(k = 0\)) taken zero. dichotomous items function fits Rasch model treats item difficulty \(\delta_i = -\beta_i\) single threshold, recovering standard two-category logistic ICC. colour mapping uses scale_color_viridis_c() integer category value, natural ordering response categories preserved visually — low categories one end palette, high categories . category_labels provided, legend uses labels (e.g., "Never" / "Sometimes" / "Often") colour mapping stays integer category value. Items fewer response categories maximum (e.g., otherwise four-category scale one three-category item) contribute categories actually facet — y-axis still spans \([0, 1]\).

Model is still fit on all four   # items; `item` picks which one to plot.   if (requireNamespace("geomtextpath", quietly = TRUE)) {     RMitemCatProb(       pcmdat2,       category_labels = c("Never", "Sometimes", "Often"),       label_curves    = "path",       item            = "I1"     )   } }  # }

title: Item-Threshold Hierarchy Plot — RMitemHierarchy
text: Threshold locations centred grand mean thresholds across items, dashed reference line 0 represents mean threshold location across scale. Confidence intervals around thresholds 84% default (sem_multiplier = 1.405), following Payton, Greenstone, & Schenker (2003) – non-overlap 84% intervals approximately corresponds two-sample significance test \(\alpha = 0.05\). Use sem_multiplier = 1.96 95% intervals. Polytomous . Dichotomous items single threshold coincides item location; hierarchy plot visually degenerate case. dichotomous data use RMtargeting() RMscoreSE() instead. Centring convention. PCM thresholds returned eRm::thresholds() shifted grand mean zero; item's location mean centred thresholds. dashed horizontal reference line plot marks zero – .e., average threshold across items.

title: Item-Threshold Hierarchy Plot — RMitemHierarchy

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMitemInfit
text: Infit MSQ weighted fit statistic emphasises deviations near item location. Values close 1.0 indicate good fit. Values substantially 1.0 suggest underfit (unexpected responses), values substantially 1.0 suggest overfit (overly predictable responses). definition "substantially" depends several factors sample size, needs determined simulation using RMitemInfitCutoff. general rule--thumb value correct. Conditional infit MSQ statistics computed via iarm::out_infit(), uses conditional distribution sufficient statistics (Müller, 2020). complete cases (rows without NA) used conditional fit calculation. dichotomous data (maximum score = 1), Rasch model fitted via eRm::RM(). Item locations negative beta parameters. Person locations estimated via eRm::person.parameter(). polytomous data (maximum score > 1), Partial Credit Model fitted via eRm::PCM(). Item average locations taken "Location" column threshold parameter table returned eRm::thresholds(); column absent, row means threshold columns used instead. Person locations estimated via eRm::person.parameter(). Relative item location defined item's average location minus sample mean person location, providing measure item targeting. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

code: # \donttest{ # Simulate binary item response data (5 items, 40 persons) set.seed(42) sim_data <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0:1, 40 * 5, replace = TRUE), nrow = 40, ncol = 5) ) colnames(sim_data) <- paste0("Item", 1:5)  # Default kable output RMitemInfit(sim_data) #>  #>  #> Table: MSQ values based on conditional estimation (n = 40 complete cases). #>  #> |Item  | Infit MSQ| Relative location| #> |:-----|---------:|-----------------:| #> |Item1 |     1.049|             -0.03| #> |Item2 |     0.929|             -0.54| #> |Item3 |     0.828|             -0.23| #> |Item4 |     1.216|              0.26| #> |Item5 |     0.931|             -0.76|  # Sorted by infit MSQ descending RMitemInfit(sim_data, sort = "infit") #>  #>  #> Table: MSQ values based on conditional estimation (n = 40 complete cases). #>  #> |Item  | Infit MSQ| Relative location| #> |:-----|---------:|-----------------:| #> |Item4 |     1.216|              0.26| #> |Item1 |     1.049|             -0.03| #> |Item5 |     0.931|             -0.76| #> |Item2 |     0.929|             -0.54| #> |Item3 |     0.828|             -0.23|  # Return as data.frame for further processing df <- RMitemInfit(sim_data, output = "dataframe") # } # \donttest{ # Simulation-based cutoffs (100 Monte-Carlo iterations) cutoff_res <- RMitemInfitCutoff(sim_data, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                             seed = 42) RMitemInfit(sim_data, cutoff = cutoff_res) #>  #>  #> Table: MSQ values based on conditional estimation (n = 40 complete cases). Cutoff values based on 99 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI). #>  #> |Item  | Infit MSQ| Infit low| Infit high|Flagged | Relative location| #> |:-----|---------:|---------:|----------:|:-------|-----------------:| #> |Item1 |     1.049|     0.576|      1.489|FALSE   |             -0.03| #> |Item2 |     0.929|     0.660|      1.537|FALSE   |             -0.54| #> |Item3 |     0.828|     0.547|      1.374|FALSE   |             -0.23| #> |Item4 |     1.216|     0.699|      1.349|FALSE   |              0.26| #> |Item5 |     0.931|     0.689|      1.315|FALSE   |             -0.76| RMitemInfit(sim_data, cutoff = cutoff_res, output = "dataframe") #>    Item Infit_MSQ Infit_low Infit_high Flagged Relative_location #> 1 Item1     1.049     0.576      1.489   FALSE             -0.03 #> 2 Item2     0.929     0.660      1.537   FALSE             -0.54 #> 3 Item3     0.828     0.547      1.374   FALSE             -0.23 #> 4 Item4     1.216     0.699      1.349   FALSE              0.26 #> 5 Item5     0.931     0.689      1.315   FALSE             -0.76 # }

title: Simulation-Based Q3 Cutoff Determination — RMlocdepQ3Cutoff
text: Uses parametric bootstrap simulation determine appropriate cutoff value RMlocdepQ3. correctly fitting Rasch model, Q3 residuals unknown distribution; function simulates distribution returns empirical percentiles.

title: Simulation-Based Q3 Cutoff Determination — RMlocdepQ3Cutoff
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). iterations Integer. Number simulation iterations (default 500). parallel Logical. Use parallel processing via mirai available (default TRUE). n_cores Integer NULL. Number parallel workers. NULL, getOption("mc.cores") checked first. neither set parallel = TRUE, warning issued execution falls back sequential (single core) processing. verbose Logical. Show progress bar (default FALSE). seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. cutoff_method Character. Method used compute per-pair Q3 credible intervals pair_cutoffs. One "hdci" (default, Highest Density Continuous Interval via ggdist::hdci()) "quantile" (symmetric 2.5th / 97.5th percentiles). affects pair_cutoffs; global $suggested_cutoff (99th percentile max(Q3) - mean(Q3)) unaffected. hdci_width Numeric (0, 1). Width HDCI cutoff_method = "hdci". Default 0.99. Ignored cutoff_method = "quantile".

title: Simulation-Based Q3 Cutoff Determination — RMlocdepQ3Cutoff
text: list components: results data.frame columns mean, max, diff (one row per successful iteration). pair_results Long data.frame columns Item1, Item2, Q3, iteration — one row per item pair per successful iteration. Used RMlocdepQ3Plot. pair_cutoffs data.frame per-pair cutoff summaries: Item1, Item2, Q3_low, Q3_high. Boundaries computed via method specified cutoff_method. actual_iterations Number successful iterations. sample_n Number persons original data. sample_summary Summary statistics estimated person parameters. item_names Character vector item names data. max_diff, sd_diff Max SD diff distribution. p95, p99, p995, p999 Empirical percentiles diff. suggested_cutoff 99th percentile (p99) — recommended scalar cutoff RMlocdepQ3. cutoff_method method used pair_cutoffs ("hdci" "quantile"). hdci_width HDCI width used (meaningful cutoff_method = "hdci").

title: Simulation-Based Q3 Cutoff Determination — RMlocdepQ3Cutoff
text: simulation iteration, person parameters (thetas) resampled replacement ML estimates, response data simulated Rasch model, mirt Rasch model fitted simulated data, Q3 residuals extracted. distribution max(Q3) - mean(Q3) across iterations provides empirical critical values. Failed iterations (e.g., due convergence issues) silently discarded. Supports dichotomous data (via eRm::RM() psychotools::rrm()) polytomous data (via eRm::PCM() internal partial credit score simulator). Parallel processing provided mirai package (optional). Install install.packages("mirai") enable parallelisation.

what: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: Computes three reliability indices Rasch / partial credit model: Person Separation Index (PSI) via eRm::SepRel(), empirical reliability via mirt::empirical_rxx(), Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) via RMUreliability() applied plausible values mirt::fscores().

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
code: RMreliability(   data,   conf_int = 0.95,   draws = 1000,   rmu_iter = 50,   estim = "WLE",   boot = FALSE,   boot_iter = 200,   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   theta_range = c(-10, 10),   output = "kable" )

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). conf_int Numeric (0, 1). HDCI width bootstrap CIs RMU. Default 0.95. draws Integer. Number plausible-value draws drawn mirt model RMU calculation. Default 1000. gives stable RMU; computational cost mostly linear. rmu_iter Integer. Number times RMUreliability() repeated set plausible-value draws (repetition uses fresh random column split). Estimates averaged across repetitions stabilise split-induced variability. Default 50. estim Character. Theta estimator used mirt::fscores() empirical reliability plausible-value seed. One "WLE" (default), "EAP", "MAP", "ML". Plausible draws produced Metropolis-Hastings. boot Logical. TRUE, run non-parametric bootstrap obtain CIs PSI Empirical reliability. Default FALSE. boot_iter Integer. Number bootstrap iterations boot = TRUE. Default 200. parallel Logical. Use parallel processing via mirai bootstrap available. Default TRUE. n_cores Integer NULL. Number parallel workers. NULL, getOption("mc.cores") checked first; neither set, bootstrapping falls back sequential. seed Integer NULL. Master random seed reproducibility. verbose Logical. Print progress messages progress bar bootstrap. Default FALSE. theta_range Numeric length-2 vector. Theta limits passed mirt::fscores(). Default c(-10, 10). output Character. "kable" (default) formatted knitr::kable() table, "dataframe" underlying data.frame.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: output = "kable": knitr_kable object one row per metric. output = "dataframe": data.frame columns metric, estimate, lower, upper, notes.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: Confidence intervals PSI Empirical reliability obtained non-parametric bootstrap (refitting eRm mirt models resample). RMU interval HDCI correlations across plausible-value draws, averaged rmu_iter random splits draws. CRAN-friendly replacement easyRasch::RIreliability(). TAM-based plausible-values path removed; mirt MML backend. dichotomous data (max = 1), mirt::mirt(..., itemtype = "Rasch") eRm::RM() fitted. polytomous data, itemtype used mirt eRm::PCM() eRm. PSI computed eRm::SepRel() person-parameter object eRm::person.parameter(). Note PSI excludes respondents extreme (min/max) raw scores construction. RMU Bignardi, Kievit, & Bürkner (2025), modified use mirt plausible values rather fully Bayesian posterior draws (see Mislevy, 1991, plausible-values framework). Bootstrap iterations fail converge either mirt eRm silently dropped.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability

Randomization-Based Inference Latent Variables Complex Samples. Psychometrika, 56(2), 177-196. doi:10.1007/BF02294457 Adams, R. J. (2005). Reliability measurement design effect. Studies Educational Evaluation, 31(2), 162-172. doi:10.1016/j.stueduc.2005.05.008

title: Raw-Score to Logit Score Transformation Table — RMscoreSE
text: function automatically detects whether data dichotomous (max score 1) polytomous (max score > 1) selects appropriate Rasch / Partial Credit model. method = "WLE" fits model eRm::RM() eRm::PCM() (CML) applies Warm's Weighted Likelihood correction bias inherent MLE-based person estimates score boundaries. Score-boundary estimates obtained via theta_range search; boundary solved within range returned Inf / -Inf NA SE. path uses lightly patched copies iarm 0.4.x person-estimation code boundary scores converge cleanly across full theta range. method = "EAP" fits model mirt::mirt(..., itemtype = "Rasch") (MML) obtains sum-score-based EAP estimates posterior SDs via mirt::fscores(method = "EAPsum", full.scores = FALSE, full.scores.SE = TRUE). EAP estimates finite score boundaries (prior shrinks inward), depend assumed normal prior theta. Item parameters MML differ slightly CML values used WLE path; well-behaved data difference small.

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). Missing values (NA) allowed. robust Logical. FALSE (default), histogram annotations use mean ± SD. TRUE, median ± MAD used instead. sort_items Character string controlling item ordering y-axis bottom panel. "data" (default) preserves column order data (first item top). "location" sorts items average threshold location (easiest top, hardest bottom). bins Integer. Number bins histograms. Default number unique scores plus one, less 15. xlim Numeric vector length 2. Initial lower upper limits shared x-axis. Automatically expanded person item threshold values fall outside limits. ci_level Numeric. Confidence level item threshold error bars. Default 0.95 (95% CI). Set NULL hide error bars. person_fill Fill colour person histogram. Default "#0072B2" (blue). threshold_fill Fill colour item threshold histogram. Default "#D55E00" (vermillion). height_ratios Numeric vector length 3 specifying relative heights top (person), middle (threshold), bottom (dot-whisker) panels. Default c(3, 2, 5). output Character string. "figure" (default) returns combined patchwork plot. "list" returns named list three ggplot objects (p1, p2, p3) customisation.

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: output = "figure": patchwork object (combined ggplot). output = "list": named list elements p1 (person histogram), p2 (threshold histogram), p3 (item threshold dot-whisker plot).

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: Together, top middle panels form back--back histogram makes easy assess whether test well-targeted sample. Estimation method selection. function checks whether item response category fewer 3 observations. categories least 3 responses, item threshold locations standard errors estimated via Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) using eRm::RM() (dichotomous) eRm::PCM() (polytomous), SEs eRm::thresholds(). category fewer 3 responses, function falls back Marginal Maximum Likelihood (MML) estimation via mirt::mirt() itemtype = "Rasch" SE = TRUE, numerically stable sparse-category conditions. message emitted MML fallback used. cases, item threshold locations centered (shifted grand mean thresholds equals zero). Person estimates obtained via Maximum Likelihood (ML) eRm::person.parameter(), uses spline interpolation extrapolate location estimates persons extreme scores (-zero perfect). Persons spline interpolation fails receive NA excluded histogram. Confidence intervals item thresholds based Wald-type intervals: threshold estimate ± z × SE, z standard normal quantile corresponding ci_level. ggplot2 patchwork packages must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: Wright, B. D. & Stone, M. H. (1979). Best Test Design. MESA Press.

code: # \donttest{ # Polytomous example set.seed(42) sim_data <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0:3, 200 * 8, replace = TRUE), nrow = 200, ncol = 8) ) colnames(sim_data) <- paste0("Item", 1:8)  # Default: mean/SD, data order, 95% CI RMtargeting(sim_data)   # Robust (median/MAD), sorted by location, 84% CI RMtargeting(sim_data, robust = TRUE, sort_items = "location", ci_level = 0.84)   # Get list of sub-plots for customisation plots <- RMtargeting(sim_data, output = "list") plots$p1 + ggplot2::ggtitle("My custom title")   # Dichotomous example sim_bin <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0:1, 200 * 10, replace = TRUE), nrow = 200, ncol = 10) ) colnames(sim_bin) <- paste0("Item", 1:10) RMtargeting(sim_bin)  # }

what: easyRasch2: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R — easyRasch2-package
title: easyRasch2: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R — easyRasch2-package
text: Streamlines reproducible Rasch measurement theory analyses ordinal item-response data, combining estimation routines 'eRm', 'psychotools', 'mirt', 'iarm', 'lavaan' consistent diagnostic, plotting, reporting layers. Covers four basic psychometric criteria summarised Christensen et al. (2021) doi:10.1111/sms.13908  – unidimensionality, local independence, ordered response category thresholds, invariance across subgroups – together item fit, targeting, reliability, category functioning, descriptive item-response plots. distinguishing feature use simulation-based critical values replace rule--thumb cutoffs conditional infit mean-square, Yen's Q3 local-dependence statistic, largest residual-PCA eigenvalue, ordinal CFA fit indices. Outputs 'knitr::kable()' tables 'ggplot2' figures suitable direct inclusion 'Quarto' 'R Markdown' reports.

title: easyRasch2 0.8.0
text: Optional label_curves = "path" mode (requires optional geomtextpath package, Suggests) writes category’s label along curve classic IRT trace-plot style. Single-item — pass full multi-item dataset use item argument choose item’s curves plot. model still fit items (CML threshold estimation needs full dataset); rendering filtered. category’s label positioned curve’s modal theta (peak bell middle categories; relevant edge monotone extreme categories), clamped small margin plot edges prevent clipping.

title: easyRasch2 0.8.0
text: release renames 22 exported functions consistent domain-prefix → method → variant-suffix scheme autocompletion prefix (RMdif, RMlocdep, RMitem, RMdim, RMplot) surfaces every related function. semantic changes; names. complete mapping new ?easyRasch2-renaming help page. short version: Unchanged: RMreliability(), RMUreliability(), RMtargeting(), RMscoreSE(). Breaking change. deprecation aliases shipped — existing scripts need search--replace. See ?easyRasch2-renaming full table.

title: easyRasch2 0.7.1
text: New RMlocdepQ3plot() visualising per-pair simulated Q3 distributions observed Q3 values, mirroring design RMpgLDplot() RMpgDIFplot(). Supports items n_pairs filters; data supplied, ranks n_pairs |observed Q3 - median(simulated Q3 per pair)|. RMlocdepQ3cutoff() now retains per-pair iteration-level data: returned list gains pair_results, pair_cutoffs, item_names, cutoff_method, hdci_width. Existing scalar outputs (suggested_cutoff etc.) unchanged. New arguments cutoff_method ("hdci" / "quantile") hdci_width (default 0.99) control per-pair credible intervals computed. RMlocdepQ3() can now called full list returned RMlocdepQ3cutoff() (auto-extracts $suggested_cutoff), matching partial-gamma family convention. Numeric scalar still works. RMpartgamLD() RMpgLDplot() gain n_pairs argument keep top-N pairs |gamma| (LD) |observed - median(sim)| deviation (plot). RMpartgamLD(), RMpartgamDIF(), RMitemrestscore() now use consistent column headers: Adj. p-value (BH) new p-value sign. star-string column. RMitemrestscore()’s former Absolute_difference column now signed Difference (observed − expected), - underfit visually distinguishable glance.

title: easyRasch2 0.7.0
text: New RMbarplot() RMstackedbarplot() join RMtileplot() easy visualization item response data distributions. New RMciccPlot() conditional item characteristic curves plot, also includes DIF analysis categorical DIF variables. New RMitemHierarchy(), outputs plot illustrating item hierarchy item thresholds confidence intervals. Modified RMcfaCutoff() use .scaled metrics RMSEA CFI, stability. See documentation details. Bug fix RMmartinLof() dichotomous items. Bug fix RMitemHierarchy() making option item_labels work intended. Tests written functions, preparing CRAN submission nearish future. Drafted package intro/vignette

title: easyRasch2 0.5.0

Set cutoff_method = "quantile" restore previous behaviour (2.5th/97.5th percentiles). ggdist package required cutoff_method = "hdci" (added Suggests). returned list now also includes cutoff_method hdci_width fields. RMiteminfit() caption updated: cutoff return value RMinfitcutoff(), kable caption now states cutoff method, e.g. "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI)." title: easyRasch2 0.4.0
text: RMiteminfit() gains optional cutoff parameter: Accepts return value RMinfitcutoff() ($item_cutoffs data.frame directly). provided, per-item cutoff boundaries (Infit_low, Infit_high) logical Flagged column added "dataframe" "kable" output. kable caption includes number simulation iterations available. New RMinfitcutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination [RMiteminfit()]. Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). Returns per-item 2.5th 97.5th percentiles simulated infit outfit MSQ distributions ($item_cutoffs), together full iteration-level results ($results). Requires iarm package (Suggests).

title: easyRasch2 0.3.0
text: New RMiteminfit(): Computes conditional infit MSQ statistics item via iarm::out_infit(), enriched item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "infit" sorts infit MSQ descending. complete cases used conditional fit calculation. New RMitemrestscore(): Computes observed model-expected item-restscore correlations via iarm::item_restscore(), enriched absolute differences observed expected values, item average locations, item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "diff" sorts absolute difference descending.

title: easyRasch2 0.2.0
text: RMlocdepQ3(): cutoff now optional (default NULL). omitted, raw Q3 residual correlation matrix returned without dynamic cut-applied. provided, dynamic cut-(mean Q3 + cutoff) shown kable caption . Supersedes requirement always supply cutoff value (PRs #2 #3). New RMlocdepQ3cutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination RMlocdepQ3(). Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). $suggested_cutoff 99th percentile simulated Q3 max–mean distribution. New internal helpers R/utils-simulation.R: sim_poly_item(), sim_partial_score(), extract_item_thresholds(). Dependencies: eRm, psychotools (>= 0.7-3), parallel, utils moved/added Imports. mirai added Suggests.

title: easyRasch2 0.1.0
